description_long: This is an upright, arched gravestone made of weathered gray stone, partially embedded in a patch of green moss and grass at its base. The front face features an engraved relief at the top, depicting an angelic or religious scene, and below it, inscribed text commemorating William Foster, who died on October 7, 1856, at the age of 59. The stone surface shows signs of aging, with some discoloration and moss growth, especially near the bottom. The back of the gravestone is plain and covered with more moss and lichen, lacking any inscriptions or decorative elements. The sides are thin and reveal the stone's thickness, with one side showing more weathering and moss than the other. The overall appearance is solemn and historic, typical of 19th-century cemetery headstones.
description: A weathered, arched gray stone gravestone with engraved text and a relief, partially covered in moss and grass at the base.
description_view_0: The front view shows the engraved relief and inscription, with moss and grass at the base.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the thin profile of the gravestone and moss growth along the bottom edge.
description_view_2: The back view is plain, heavily weathered, and covered with moss and lichen, with no inscriptions.
description_view_3: The right side view shows the clean stone edge with minimal moss, highlighting the stone's thickness.

license_info.creator_display_name: Santa Cruz Museum of Art and History
